conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex, and he's 17 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me start by asking: Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with him and are you responsible for his care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for Alex. He lives at home with me and I take care of him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. 

Since Alex is 17 years old, I need to ask: Is Alex currently in approved education or training? This typically includes things like full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar qualifications) or approved training programs.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He left school and isn't studying anything right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Let me read the official guidance to confirm the eligibility rules before giving you my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Based on the official guidance and the information you've provided, I now have everything I need to make an assessment.

Let me explain the situation:

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. However, for children aged 16 or over, you'll only still qualify if your child is **under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training**.

Since Alex is 17 years old and is not currently in any education or training, unfortunately you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for him under the current rules.

The only exception would be if Alex had recently left education or training and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services - in which case Child Benefit can continue for 20 weeks. However, based on what you've told me, this doesn't appear to apply to your situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but based on the current circumstances, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

The reason is that Alex is 17 years old and not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit for children aged 16 or over only continues if they remain in approved education or training and are under 20 years old.

If Alex's circumstances change and he enters approved education or training in the future, you would be able to claim Child Benefit for him at that point (as long as he remains under 20).

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old. According to Child Benefit rules, while the claimant is responsible for Alex and lives with him, eligibility for children aged 16 or over requires that the child is under 20 AND stays in approved education or training. Since Alex is not currently in any education or training, the claimant does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (aged 17), who lives at home. However, Alex is not in approved education or training, which is a requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16 or over. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
